# Sprint Change Proposal — 2026-04-25 (v4, substantive scope addition)

**Trigger:** Authorised major scope addition driven by today's market-research → PRD-amendment → architecture-evaluation chain
**Generated by:** `bmad-correct-course` workflow (batch mode, Auto Mode active)
**Project:** EU Solicit
**Scope Classification:** **Major** — 8 net-new epics (~28 stories), 1 net-new service, +€250–600/mo run-rate, +€50K one-time ISO 27001 audit budget. v5 architecture survives intact.
**Author:** Deb (facilitated by the BMAD agent chain — Mary → Winston → this proposal)
**Approval:** Deb signed off on all six open questions on 2026-04-25 (architecture-evaluation §11)
**Relationship to prior proposals:** **Substantive — distinct from the drift-loop fires.** v1 (`sprint-change-proposal-2026-04-24.md`) document-consolidation work and v2–v8 (`sprint-change-proposal-2026-04-25*.md`) drift-loop chain remain independently open. This v4 addresses a different concern (strategic scope expansion) and produces no new conflict with that consolidation.

---

## TL;DR

Deb's strategic decision today: pivot positioning from a generic EU procurement tool to **"the only EU-native platform that wins both public tenders and EU grants for consulting firms."** Driver was a market-research finding that Altura (NL) raised €8M Series A in June 2025 with the same "EU's GovWin" positioning EU Solicit was about to claim — closing the dual-segment whitespace within 12–18 months unless EU Solicit moves first.

This proposal injects **8 epics + 1 parallel ISO 27001 audit programme** into the sprint plan, refines the dependency map, locks the six open decisions, and routes the change set to `bmad-create-epics-and-stories` for decomposition.

The v5 Solution Architecture is preserved. All patterns from Epics 1–13 extend cleanly. **Five of six PRD changes are extensions of existing patterns; only the multi-client workspace data model is genuinely new architecture.**

---

## Section 1: Issue Summary

### Trigger

A market-research study commissioned this morning (2026-04-25) revealed that EU Solicit's intended primary positioning — "EU's equivalent of GovWin IQ" for tender intelligence — is now adjacent-occupied by **Altura (NL)**:
- €8M Series A in June 2025
- ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified, EEA-hosted
- Acquired Berlin-based Tendara in July 2025 for German expansion
- Blue-chip enterprise references including EY, Adecco, Ricoh
- Explicit "autonomous AI agents for bidding" marketing

Three further findings converged the strategic decision:
1. **No competitor combines tenders + grants** (the dual-segment angle). PNO Group does both, but as a consultancy not a SaaS. This is the only structurally unique whitespace remaining.
2. **The behavioural wedge survives a CFO conversation** — bid managers spend 19% of their working time on portal scanning (~€11–15K/FTE/year addressable spend at standard loaded cost).
3. **Pricing band defensible** — GovWin's $13K minimum / $29K average per-org per-year and Loopio's $1,440/user/year list both bracket EU Solicit's planned €25–60/user/month and €199/user/month Pro+ tier as defensible.

### Problem Statement

EU Solicit's MVP plan (v1.0 PRD) is structured for a single-tenant SME-bidder ICP and lacks several capabilities that the consulting-firm ICP requires as deal-stakes:
- No multi-client workspace model (consulting firms manage 10–50 client engagements)
- No CRM integrations (consulting bid pipelines live in HubSpot/Salesforce/Pipedrive)
- No ISO 27001 commitment with target date (procurement-gate prerequisite for mid-large EU firms)
- 99.5% SLA below EU SaaS market norm (Loopio/Responsive offer 99.9%)
- No outcome telemetry (renewal-conversation engine missing)
- No per-bid SKU UX surfacing (the Stripe primitive exists in v5 but is not customer-facing)

Without closing these gaps in the next 12 months, EU Solicit cedes the consulting-firm ICP to Altura.

### Issue Categorisation

**Strategic pivot driven by market change** — not a technical limitation, requirements misunderstanding, or failed approach. The original v1.0 PRD remains coherent for an SME-bidder ICP; the decision is to *reposition* to a higher-WTP segment with concrete competitive evidence justifying the pivot.

### Evidence

- Market research artefact (1,085 lines, 25+ primary sources): `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/research/market-eu-govwin-tender-intelligence-research-2026-04-25.md`
- Competitor-funding evidence: [Tech.eu (June 2025)](https://tech.eu/2025/06/09/altura-raises-eur8m-for-ai-powered-bid-management-platform/), [Curiosity VC announcement](https://www.curiosityvc.com/post/altura-raises-eu8m-series-a-for-autonomous-ai-agents-that-help-organizations-win-public-and-private-tenders), [Altura's own Tendara-acquisition blog](https://altura.io/en/blog/altura-acquired-tendara)
- Pricing benchmarks: [Vendr GovWin IQ buyer guide](https://www.vendr.com/buyer-guides/govwin-iq), [Vendr Loopio guide](https://www.vendr.com/marketplace/loopio)
- Buyer-behaviour evidence: [Tendify — Day in the Life of a Bid Manager](https://www.tendify.eu/article/day-in-the-life-of-bid-manager) (19% time on search), [SMEunited](https://www.smeunited.eu/admin/storage/smeunited/20251215-smeunited-public-procurement.pdf) (2,000+ EU portals; single-bidder rate doubled in a decade)
- Compliance posture preference: [HST Solutions — ISO 27001 vs SOC 2 in EU procurement](https://www.hst.ie/blog/iso-27001-vs-soc-2-which-certification-do-eu-buyers-actually-require/)

---

## Section 2: Impact Analysis

### Epic Impact

**Epics already shipped (1–12):** No retroactive changes required. Patterns from these epics (TierGate, EventBus, dual-layer idempotency, Fernet OAuth tokens, two-layer resilience, RBAC factory, ATDD-first discipline, structlog stdlib routing, fire-and-forget audit writes) **extend cleanly** to all 8 new epics. The architecture evaluation (§7) confirms no rework needed.

**Epic 13 (in progress):** Continues unchanged. This v4 introduces no new requirements that affect Epic 13's drift-recovery work or the inj-01/02/03 + dw-01/02/03 sub-stories. Epic 13's known carry-forwards (Dependabot, Prometheus /metrics, k6 baseline) **converge into Epic 21** rather than creating a separate accumulation:
- **k6 baseline carry-forward** → becomes story **PE.01** in Epic 21 (now critical-path; gates 99.9% SLA publication)
- **Dependabot config** → remains in Epic 13's carry-forward set; no movement into v4 epics
- **Prometheus /metrics bootstrap** → becomes part of **PE.05** (SLO dashboards) in Epic 21

**8 net-new epics injected after Epic 13:**

| Epic | Title | Stories | Tier impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **14** | Multi-Client Workspace Data Model & RBAC | 5 (S14.00–S14.04) | Foundational; new tenancy model |
| **15** | Per-Bid SKU + Pro+ Tier Launch | 3 (S15.00–S15.02) | New Pro+ tier (€199/user/month) + per-bid SKU surfacing |
| **16** | Slack/Teams Notifications | 1 (S16.00) | Pro tier feature |
| **17** | CRM Integrations (HubSpot → Pipedrive → Salesforce) | 4 (S17.00–S17.03) | Pro+ tier feature; new `integrations-api` service |
| **18** | Trust Center & Compliance Posture | 3 (S18.00–S18.02) | Public `/trust` page; M2 deadline |
| **19** | Outcome Telemetry & Renewal Proof Brief | 3 (S19.00–S19.02) | Cross-tier; renewal engine |
| **20** | NPS, Reviews & Onboarding (residual) | 1 (S20.00) | Pro+ feature |
| **21** | Platform Reliability for 99.9% SLA | 6 (PE.01–PE.06) | Cross-cutting platform workstream |

**Plus parallel programme:** ISO 27001 audit-prep (M2–M12, €50K budget, ~8–10 sprint-weeks distributed engineering effort).

### Story Impact (Detailed Sub-Story Map)

Total: **22 feature stories + 6 platform stories = 28 stories** plus the ISO programme.

**Epic 14 — Multi-Client Workspace** (5 stories, foundational):
- **S14.00:** Schema migration + atomic single-default-workspace backfill (no transition shim needed — no existing paying customers)
- **S14.01:** Workspace CRUD at tenant-admin level + audit-trail `workspace_id` extension
- **S14.02:** RBAC extension — `WorkspaceScope` Depends() factory + `tenant_admin` cross-workspace bypass
- **S14.03:** Frontend workspace switcher + URL routing `/[locale]/workspace/[workspaceId]/...` + Zustand persist namespace migration (2 ATDD-heavy sprints)
- **S14.04:** External collaborator magic-link flow (30-day expiry, no Stripe seat consumption)

**Epic 15 — Per-Bid SKU + Pro+ Tier** (3 stories):
- **S15.00:** Pro+ tier definition (€199/user/month) + `tier_access_policies` row + Stripe Price ID + tier-cache extension
- **S15.01:** Per-bid pricing tiers (€99 / €199 / €299) + Stripe Checkout integration + `add_on_pricing_tiers` table
- **S15.02:** `_USAGE_LUA` metering-bypass extension + integration tests for concurrent INCR

**Epic 16 — Slack/Teams Notifications** (1 story):
- **S16.00:** `integrations-api` service bootstrap + webhook config UI + Slack/Teams incoming-webhook templates + alert routing

**Epic 17 — CRM Integrations** (4 stories, complexity-driven order):
- **S17.00:** CRM connection model + Fernet token vault + sync engine scaffold + conflict-resolution framework (LWW + audit log)
- **S17.01:** HubSpot adapter — full bi-directional sync (Deals + Contacts)
- **S17.02:** Pipedrive adapter
- **S17.03:** Salesforce adapter (rate-limit-aware; daily quota; 6-week add)

**Epic 18 — Trust Center** (3 stories, M2 deadline):
- **S18.00:** Public `/trust` route + MDX pipeline + `infra/sub-processors.yaml` + change-log generator
- **S18.01:** PDF artefact pipeline (WeasyPrint reuse from Epic 7) for living artefacts; Git-managed for legal artefacts
- **S18.02:** Sub-processor change → DPA notification flow (notification service, new email template)

**Epic 19 — Outcome Telemetry** (3 stories):
- **S19.00:** Platform-attributed flag + bid-outcome capture UI + materialized views (`mv_workspace_outcome_stats`, `mv_workspace_content_reuse_stats`)
- **S19.01:** Outcome Dashboard frontend widgets + workspace-config overrides (hourly rate, hours-saved baseline)
- **S19.02:** Monthly Outcome Brief PDF generation + onboarding milestone tracker + CSM stall alerts (>7 days)

**Epic 20 — NPS, Reviews, Onboarding (residual)** (1 story):
- **S20.00:** Third-party NPS SDK integration (Delighted/Wootric) + opt-in routing to G2/Capterra deep links + privacy disclosure

**Epic 21 — Platform Reliability for 99.9% SLA** (6 platform-engineering stories, parallel workstream):
- **PE.01:** k6 baseline closure (carries forward from Epic 13; **critical path** for any SLO declaration)
- **PE.02:** PostgreSQL HA migration (managed RDS Multi-AZ or equivalent — boring tech)
- **PE.03:** Redis HA migration (Sentinel or managed cluster)
- **PE.04:** PodDisruptionBudgets + min-replica enforcement across all services
- **PE.05:** SLO dashboards (Prometheus + Grafana) + error-budget alerting (absorbs Epic 13 carry-forward)
- **PE.06:** On-call rotation + runbook authoring + incident-management process

### Artefact Conflicts

**PRD:** Already updated. `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/PRD.md` v1.1 incorporates all six PRD-level changes (FR8 multi-client workspace, FR9 outcome telemetry, FR10 Trust Center, FR11 CRM/Comms integrations, FR2.4–2.7 per-bid SKU + NPS, NFR §7 99.9% SLA + ISO 27001 commitment). New user stories US7–US14 added; US1–US6 carried forward with workspace-scoping notes. **No conflict with v1 consolidation work** — the PRD update went into the canonical `planning-artifacts/PRD.md` file (not creating a new variant).

**Architecture (v5 Solution Architecture):** Survives intact. Architecture evaluation §3–§5 documents net-new database tables (6 new in `client` schema, +1 nullable column on `shared.audit_log`, new `integrations` schema) and one new service (`integrations-api`). Total of 6 services post-amendment; still within the "5–10 sweet spot" the original decomposition analysis defines. ADR §1.14 (per-bid add-on as Stripe one-time charges) **already covers** the per-bid SKU primitive — this scope is mostly UX surfacing, not net-new architecture.

**UX:** No conflict with existing UX spec. Workspace switcher and `/trust` page are net-new UI additions documented in architecture-evaluation §CC-4.

**Tests/CI:** ATDD-heavy stories in Epic 14 (especially S14.03 frontend) require ~2 sprints of frontend ATDD investment. Existing test patterns (TierGate per-route Depends, transaction-rollback fixture, cross-tenant negative tests, Vitest source-inspection ATDD, Playwright tier-gate E2E) all extend cleanly per project-context.md Patterns Epic 6/7/11. Workspace-scoped negative tests become mandatory for all new endpoints (extension of existing cross-tenant pattern).

**Infrastructure (Helm/K8s):** New service requires new Helm chart, ingress route, secrets, network policy. PG HA migration (PE.02) and Redis HA migration (PE.03) are non-trivial cutovers requiring planned maintenance windows.

### Technical Impact

| Layer | Impact |
|---|---|
| **Database** | +6 tables in `client` schema, +8 columns on existing tables, +1 nullable column on `shared.audit_log`, +1 new schema (`integrations`) with 2 tables, +2 materialized views |
| **Services** | +1 net-new service (`integrations-api`); existing 5 services extended (client-api gets workspace + outcome telemetry + per-bid metering bypass; notification gets sub-processor change emails + onboarding stall alerts) |
| **Frontend** | New top-level workspace switcher; URL routing changes (`/[locale]/workspace/[workspaceId]/...`); Zustand persist key namespace migration; new `/trust` public route; NPS prompt SDK integration |
| **Infrastructure** | PG HA + Redis HA + PDBs; Stripe configuration (1 Pro+ Price ID + 3 per-bid Price IDs); Cloudflare/ingress route for `/trust` (no auth); new `integrations-api` Helm chart |
| **Compliance** | ISO 27001 audit-prep programme M2–M12 (€50K); Trust Center M2; sub-processor list version-controlled |

---

## Section 3: Recommended Approach

**Path: Direct Adjustment + Parallel Platform Workstream + Parallel Compliance Programme.**

This is **not** a rollback (no completed work to revert; Epic 13 is in progress and unaffected). This is **not** an MVP redefinition (the v1.0 MVP is still coherent — we are *expanding* scope to address a higher-WTP ICP). This **is** a major scope addition with three parallel workstreams.

### Recommended Sequencing (calendar map M0–M12)

```
                M0   M1   M2   M3   M4   M5   M6   M7   M8   M9   M10  M11  M12
                ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Epic 14         ████████████░                                   (5 stories, FE-heavy)
  S14.00,01,02      ████░                                       (backend foundation)
  S14.03                  ████░                                 (frontend, 2 sprints)
  S14.04                       ██░                              (external collab)

Epic 15                  █████░                                 (3 stories, Pro+ + per-bid)
  S15.00                  ██░
  S15.01                       ██░
  S15.02                            ██░

Epic 16                       ███░                              (1 story, Slack/Teams)

Epic 18         ██████░                                         (3 stories, M2 deadline)
  S18.00,01,02   ██████░

Epic 19                       █████░                            (3 stories, outcome)

Epic 17                            ████████████░                (4 stories, CRM)
  S17.00,01           ████░                                     (HubSpot first)
  S17.02                       ███░                             (Pipedrive)
  S17.03                            █████░                      (Salesforce, 6w)

Epic 20                             ██░                         (1 story, NPS)

Epic 21         ████████████░                                   (6 PE stories, parallel)
  PE.01           ██░  ← k6 baseline (CRITICAL PATH)
  PE.02              ████░  ← PG HA
  PE.03                  ███░  ← Redis HA
  PE.04                       ██░  ← PDBs
  PE.05                            ██░  ← SLO dashboards
  PE.06                                ██░  ← On-call

ISO 27001                  ████████████████████████████████████████░  (parallel)
  Controls inv      ███░
  Evidence + DR              ████████░
  Stage 1 audit                            ██░
  Stage 2 audit                                       ██░
  Certification                                                       ████░

Public SLA announce                                ▲ M4 — only after Epic 21 PE.01–PE.04 ship
Trust Center live          ▲ M2 — Epic 18 deadline
ISO 27001 cert awarded                                              ▲ M12
Pro+ tier launches                  ▲ M3 — after Epic 14 + 15 ship
First CRM live                       ▲ M5 (HubSpot)
First per-bid SKU sale                ▲ M3 — after S15.01
```

### Effort & Risk Summary

| Workstream | Effort (sprint-weeks) | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| Epic 14 (Multi-Client Workspace) | 6–8 | **High** — biggest single architectural change; FE refactor |
| Epic 15 (Per-Bid + Pro+) | 3 | Low — extends existing v5 patterns |
| Epic 16 (Slack/Teams) | 1 | Very Low |
| Epic 17 (CRM) | 10–12 | Medium — third-party API stability + rate limits |
| Epic 18 (Trust Center) | 3 | Low |
| Epic 19 (Outcome Telemetry) | 3 | Low |
| Epic 20 (NPS) | 1 | Very Low |
| Epic 21 (Platform 99.9%) | 6–8 | Medium — PG HA cutover risk |
| ISO 27001 programme | 8–10 distributed | Medium — auditor engagement + evidence completeness |
| **TOTAL** | **41–55 sprint-weeks** (~2 quarters at 2 engineers; ~1 quarter at 4 engineers) | — |

### Run-Rate Impact

- Managed PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (vs. single-instance): +€100–200/mo
- Managed Redis HA (vs. single-instance): +€50–100/mo
- Third-party NPS SDK (Delighted/Wootric): €100–300/mo
- New `integrations-api` pod: ~€20/mo
- **Total run-rate increase: €270–620/mo**
- **One-time:** ISO 27001 audit (€50K planning assumption per locked decisions)

### Locked Decisions (Replicated for Convenience)

| # | Decision | Locked value |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Existing paying customers from v1.0 | **None** — atomic migration in S14.00, no transition-shim required |
| 2 | Pro+ tier price | **€199/user/month** (lower end of recommended range; raise based on Phase-1 customer feedback in M9–M12) |
| 3 | Per-bid SKU pricing tiers | **€99 (standard) / €199 (with grant module) / €299 (full Enterprise feature stack)** |
| 4 | Trust Center launch deadline | **Month 2** of platform launch (Epic 18) |
| 5 | ISO 27001 audit budget | **€50K** planning assumption (auditor + remediation; surveillance year 2 separate) |
| 6 | CRM provider order | **HubSpot → Pipedrive → Salesforce** (both market-driven and complexity-driven) |

## Section 5: Implementation Handoff

### Scope Classification: **Major**

This proposal adds 8 epics, 1 net-new service, and a parallel compliance programme. Per the `bmad-correct-course` framework, Major changes route to **PM + Architect**. Both have already been engaged:

- **PM/Analyst (Mary):** Produced the market research and PRD amendment brief; updated canonical PRD to v1.1.
- **Architect (Winston):** Produced the architecture evaluation with per-change verdicts, sub-story arcs, dependency map, infra-impact estimate, and 10 newly surfaced risks. Locked all six open decisions with Deb on 2026-04-25.

### Next Routing

| Skill | Purpose | Inputs |
|---|---|---|
| **`bmad-create-epics-and-stories`** ★ next | Decompose Epics 14–21 into the formal sprint plan with story files in `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/epics/` | All four artefacts above + this proposal |
| `bmad-sprint-planning` | Generate sprint-status.yaml updates for the new epic injection | After epics-and-stories ships |
| `bmad-create-architecture` (light pass) | Optional — refresh `EU_Solicit_Solution_Architecture_v5.md` to v6 with the workspace + integrations additions documented inline rather than via amendment | Not gating; can defer to post-Epic-14 |
| `bmad-edit-prd` (light pass) | Optional — formalise the v1.1 PRD as the canonical version with no amendment-pending notes | Not gating; can defer |

### Success Criteria for Implementation

1. Epic 14–21 story files exist in `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/epics/` with the structure and ACs derived from the architecture-evaluation sub-story arcs (§2 per-change).
2. Sprint plan reflects the dependency map from architecture-evaluation §7 (Epic 14 + 18 first; Epic 21 platform parallel; ISO programme parallel).
3. PE.01 (k6 baseline closure) is sequenced as the first PE-numbered story (carries forward from Epic 13; gates 99.9% SLA).
4. Stripe configuration tickets exist for: 1 Pro+ Price ID (€199/user/month) + 3 per-bid Price IDs (€99/€199/€299).
5. ISO 27001 auditor RFP is scoped against €50K budget envelope with M3 issuance target.
6. Frontend ATDD discipline for S14.03 is documented in story ACs (workspace switcher + URL routing + Zustand namespace).

## Section 6: Carry-Forward Status

This v4 does **not** close any pre-existing carry-forward items. The following remain open and tracked separately:

- **v1 (`sprint-change-proposal-2026-04-24.md`) §4 project edit proposals** — document consolidation work (PRD/Architecture/UX variant cleanup, `planning-artifacts` vs `planning_artifacts` resolution, root-level `EU_Solicit_*.md` archival). Active owner: consolidation owner (per v3). **No conflict with v4** — this proposal's PRD/architecture edits went into the canonical `planning-artifacts/PRD.md` and `planning-artifacts/architecture-evaluation-2026-04-25.md` paths, not new variants.
- **v2–v3, v8 drift-loop fires** — orchestrator-platform issue; not a project artefact concern. Awaits ACT-V8-01 / ACT-V8-02 (kill-switch + ACT-V3-01 deployment) per v8.
- **Epic 13 carry-forwards:**
  - **k6 baseline** → re-homed as PE.01 in Epic 21 (now critical-path)
  - **Prometheus /metrics bootstrap** → absorbed into PE.05 (SLO dashboards) in Epic 21
  - **Dependabot config** → remains open in Epic 13's set; not absorbed into v4 epics
- **Carry-forwards re-homed by this proposal:** Of the v8/Epic-13 carry-forwards, two (k6, Prometheus) are now explicit Epic 21 stories. **This is a net reduction in floating debt by re-anchoring it to a concrete sprint with calendar deadlines.**

| 4.1 | Direct Adjustment viable | ✅ Viable | Effort: High (41–55 sprint-weeks). Risk: Medium (Epic 14 FE refactor + PG HA cutover are highest items, both manageable). **CHOSEN PATH.** |
| 4.2 | Rollback viable | [N/A] Not viable | No completed work needs reverting; this is additive scope, not a course-correction on a failed approach |
| 4.3 | MVP review viable | [N/A] Not chosen | The v1.0 MVP remains coherent for an SME-bidder ICP. The decision is to *expand* MVP scope to address a higher-WTP ICP, not to *reduce* MVP scope |
| 5.x | Edit proposal generation | ✅ Done | All edits delegated to upstream artefacts (PRD v1.1, amendment brief, architecture evaluation). This proposal references rather than duplicates |
| 6.x | Handoff | ✅ Done | Routes to `bmad-create-epics-and-stories` per Section 5 |
